title: Epidendrum angustatum
taxonomy: Epidendrum angustatum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Epidendrum is a large and widespread group of orchids, native to the Americas. Epidendrum angustatum is further classified as a species within this genus, characterized by its distinctive flowers and growth habits. Its classification also places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae, which includes many other orchid species.

intro: Epidendrum angustatum, commonly known as the Panama orchid, is a type of epiphytic orchid that thrives in tropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of E. angustatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, E. angustatum is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or infused oil, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, bronchitis, and skin irritations.
